qa_pairs.question: Are commercial software packages available to all users externally?
qa_pairs.answer: No, commercial software packages are only available to authorized users according to the terms of the license owners and are not available externally.

qa_pairs.question: What types of software will not be granted access to by following the instructions on this page?
qa_pairs.answer: Following the instructions on this page will not grant you access to commercial software packages that are restricted to authorized users.

qa_pairs.question: Can I access Intel and Portland Group compilers by following these instructions?
qa_pairs.answer: While the modules for the Intel and PGI compilers are available, you will only have access to their redistributable parts, typically shared objects, not the full compilers themselves, so you cannot compile new software with them.

qa_pairs.question: What parts of the Intel and Portland Group compilers are accessible?
qa_pairs.answer: Only the redistributable parts of these packages, usually the shared objects, are accessible.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the available redistributable parts of commercial compilers like Intel and PGI?
qa_pairs.answer: The available redistributable parts (shared objects) are sufficient to run software packages that have already been compiled with these compilers.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to compile new software using the available modules for Intel and PGI compilers?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the available redistributable parts of these compilers are not sufficient to compile new software.

qa_pairs.question: Who can access commercial software packages?
qa_pairs.answer: Commercial software packages are made available to authorized users according to the terms of the license owners.
